title: The Three Little Pigs
style_guide: Warm, polished children's picture-book illustration with soft daylight, gentle watercolor-and-gouache texture, rounded expressive characters, cozy countryside backgrounds, consistent camera distance, and bright but natural colors. Keep the art playful and safe for young readers.
character_guide: Three young pig brothers should remain consistent across pages: the silly first pig wears a straw hat and blue overalls; the relaxed second pig wears a plaid shirt or work shirt; the smart third pig wears a yellow construction hard hat, round black glasses, and neat work clothes. The wolf is gray, lean, expressive, and mischievous rather than frightening. Keep the brick house red-orange with a chimney and the same cozy cottage feel once established.
image_constraints: Use one clean full-bleed illustration per page. Keep a 4:3-ish picture-book composition. Do not include title lettering, page numbers, speech bubbles, captions, watermarks, UI, or extra typography inside the image.

consistency_review.issue: The active page 10 image had embedded title text and a page number, unlike every other illustration.
consistency_review.action: Use the cleaner alternate page-10_1-img-01.png for page 10.

consistency_review.issue: The mother pig appears only on page 1, which is narratively fine, but her larger scale and apron make the opening image feel slightly different from the rest.
The two running pigs are shown from behind and simplified, so their clothing identities are less clear than in adjacent pages.
The final page includes a speech bubble. Future regenerated images should avoid in-image text and let the HTML carry all story text.
All current source images share the same 1800x1352 dimensions and broadly compatible warm storybook lighting.
